title: clematis parviloba subsp. bartlettii
intro: Clematis parviloba subsp. bartlettii, also known as the Bartlett clematis, is a flowering plant species in the Ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The Bartlett clematis is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a potential remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations often include infusions or tinctures of the plant, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
